perturbation.original_text: One party hereto ("Owner") may disclose to the other party hereto ("Recipient") certain Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement which Owner deems proprietary and confidential. The Parties agree that Recipient shall not use and shall prevent the disclosure of any information it receives from Owner that is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL, or similarly marked, or any other information (whether delivered in writing or verbally) which by its nature would be reasonably considered as confidential, to any other person, firm or corporation or delivered in connection with the evaluation of the Transaction, except as provided herein, and shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure of such information as Recipient employs with respect to its own Confidential Information of like importance.
perturbation.changed_text: One party hereto ("Owner") may disclose to the other party hereto ("Recipient") certain Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement which Owner deems proprietary and confidential. The Parties agree that Recipient shall not use any information it receives from Owner that is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL, or similarly marked, to any other person, firm or corporation, except as provided herein, and shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure of such information as Recipient employs with respect to its own Confidential Information of like importance.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and shall prevent the disclosure of any other information (whether delivered in writing or verbally) which by its nature would be reasonably considered as confidential or delivered in connection with the evaluation of the Transaction', Recipient now has no obligation to prevent the disclosure of verbally delivered information. However, later in Section 3, the agreement states that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information if it was already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, implying that some verbally transmitted data should be considered confidential. This creates a contradiction because the changed text reduces the prevention obligation only to marked Confidential Information, but Section 3 still implies verbally or in writing information can be deemed confidential, but not protected by the agreement..
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereto agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information, and Recipient shall have no obligation with respect to any such information which: (i) was generally known to the public prior to the disclosure under this Agreement; 1 <PAGE> (ii) is already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, as evidenced by the written electronically stored records of the Party dated prior to October 27, 1999; or (iii) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient or any person to whom the Recipient discloses such information; or (iv) is received by a third party without breach of this Agreement or any other obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (v) is independently developed by Recipient; or (vi) is approved for release by written authorization of Owner; or (vii) is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement or request of a governmental agency, or disclosure is required by operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligated the Recipient to prevent disclosure of marked confidential information AND any other information reasonably considered confidential. The changed text removes the obligation to prevent disclosure of information reasonably considered confidential, only obligating the prevention of marked confidential information. The contradicted text states that certain information (including information known to the Recipient before a specific date) is NOT considered Confidential Information, implying that other information (which could be verbally transmitted) CAN be Confidential Information, and therefore should be protected by the agreement. Since the Changed Text removes the obligation to prevent the disclosure of verbally transmitted information, while the contradicted text implies that verbally transmitted information could be deemed confidential, there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Paragraph 1 above, the Parties may disclose the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement to their employees and/or agents, but only for the purpose of supplying the Party with sufficient information to enable the Party to evaluate the potential value of establishing a business and/or contractual relationship with each other. The Recipient will inform each of its employees, agents and affiliates (collectively, "Representatives") who will receive Confidential Information of the obligations under this Agreement and agrees to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from taking any action that would constitute a breach of the terms of this Agreement. In any event, the Recipient shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Paragraph 1 above, the Parties may disclose the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement to their employees and/or agents, but only for the purpose of supplying the Party with sufficient information to enable the Party to evaluate the potential value of establishing a business and/or contractual relationship with each other. The Recipient will inform each of its employees, agents and affiliates (collectively, "Representatives") who will receive Confidential Information of the obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The omission 'and agrees to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from taking any action that would constitute a breach of the terms of this Agreement. In any event, the Recipient shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.' removes the direct liability of the Recipient for breaches by its Representatives. However, Section 12, states 'Without impairing any other provision hereof, each Party hereto will promptly advise the other of any breaches of this Agreement.' This creates an inconsistency, because while Section 2 removes direct liability, Section 12 still expects each party to advise of any breaches, implying that a breach made by the Recipient's representatives still affects the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without impairing any other provision hereof, each Party hereto will promptly advise the other of any breaches of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text held the Recipient responsible for breaches by its Representatives, and required them to take commercially reasonable measures to prevent such breaches. The changed text removes the direct liability of the Recipient for breaches by its Representatives. The contradicted text states 'each Party hereto will promptly advise the other of any breaches of this Agreement.' By removing the Recipient's responsibility for its representatives, but still requiring them to advise on breaches, an inconsistency is created. If the Recipient isn't liable for the representative's breaches, it contradicts the requirement to report those breaches, because the original text implies that a breach made by the Recipient's representatives affects the Recipient.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Parties agrees, unless otherwise required by law, not to disclose to any other person the fact that the Confidential information has been made available to the other Party, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction between the Parties, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof).
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Parties agrees, unless otherwise required by law, not to disclose to any other person any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof).
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'the fact that the Confidential information has been made available to the other Party, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction between the Parties,', it creates an in-text contradiction. Now a party may disclose that Confidential Information has been made available to the other party or that discussions or negotiations are taking place. But section 11 States that Neither Party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information and neither Party shall have any liability to the other resulting from any use of the Confidential Information which is consistent with this Agreement. If it can be publicly disclosed as a fact that Confidential Information has been made available, or Discussions are taking place, then the accuracy can be assumed, and create some liability, because it will have to match.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information and neither Party shall have any liability to the other resulting from any use of the Confidential Information which is consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented the disclosure of the fact that confidential information was made available and that discussions/negotiations were taking place. The changed text removes this prohibition, allowing such disclosure. The contradicted text states that neither party makes warranties about the accuracy of the confidential information, nor has liability for its use consistent with the agreement. The explanation posits that allowing disclosure implies some level of accuracy which could create liability, contradicting the 'no liability' clause. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is created, because by ommiting, it causes a change that is not compatible with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument duly executed by each of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended by any instrument duly executed by each of the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'written', the text now states This Agreement may be amended by any instrument duly executed by each of the Parties. Section 9, used to mandate that amendment be only written, making unwritten amendments a breach. Removing the need for it to be written, it creates the implication that written and unwritten agreements are valid, This then conflicts with section 12, which states 'Without impairing any other provision hereof, each Party hereto will promptly advise the other of any breaches of this Agreement.', since now the amendment type may be unwritten, and not advised as a breach
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without impairing any other provision hereof, each Party hereto will promptly advise the other of any breaches of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments must be in writing. The changed text removes the word 'written', implying that amendments can be in any form, including unwritten. The contradicted text states that breaches of the agreement must be promptly advised. If amendments can now be unwritten, it is unclear if unwritten amendments must be advised as a breach. This directly breaks the consistency and introduces ambiguity, adhering to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective from and after the date set forth below and shall terminate eighteen months thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective from and after the date set forth below and shall terminate months thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'eighteen' from 'terminate eighteen months thereafter,' the duration of the agreement becomes ambiguous, conflicting with the clear intent for a set term. This creates uncertainty and potential legal disputes. Without the 'eighteen', the phrase 'terminate months thereafter' implies a duration, but the length is now undefined, this can now contradict any implied month count or a year since the original text specifically provided an amount.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without impairing any other provision hereof, each Party hereto will promptly advise the other of any breaches of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a clear termination date (18 months). The changed text removes 'eighteen', making the termination date ambiguous ('terminate months thereafter'). This contradicts the contradicted text ('each Party hereto will promptly advise the other of any breaches of this Agreement') because it's now unclear when the agreement ends, making it hard to define 'breaches' relative to an ambiguous termination date, aligning with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition. Without a fixed end date, enforcement of any breach becomes questionable. If the agreement is vague, then the duration and enforcement are in conflict.
